Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 141 upvotes, 16 comments
  2. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 284 upvotes, 50 comments
  3. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 74 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Kyushu Railway Company Train Varieties 82 upvotes, 11 comments
  5. HD Audio Driver for Windows 98SE / Me 31 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Interview: Nobonoko, Master of the Minimal Sequencer 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. About the Atmosphere 48 upvotes, 8 comments
  8. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 365 upvotes, 110 comments
  9. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 130 upvotes, 35 comments
  11. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 502 upvotes, 188 comments
  12. 15 years, one server, 8GB RAM and 500k users – how Webminal refuses to die 38 upvotes, 8 comments
  13. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 207 upvotes, 72 comments
  14. Ohm's Peg-to-WASM Compiler 53 upvotes, 16 comments
  15. The Cognitive Dark Forest 399 upvotes, 181 comments
  16. More on Version Control 71 upvotes, 25 comments
  17. New Apple Silicon M4 and M5 HiDPI Limitation on 4K External Displays 240 upvotes, 113 comments
  18. Eclipse GlassFish: This Isn't Your Father's GlassFish 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  19. Hardware Image Compression 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  20. The road signs that teach travellers about France 86 upvotes, 39 comments
  21. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 549 upvotes, 357 comments
  22. "Roadrunner": a bipedal, wheeled robot for multi-modal locomotion [video] 22 upvotes, 8 comments
  23. VHDL's Crown Jewel 32 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. Claude Code runs Git reset –hard origin/main against project repo every 10 mins 238 upvotes, 164 comments
  25. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 217 upvotes, 175 comments
  26. Coding Agents Could Make Free Software Matter Again 156 upvotes, 135 comments
  27. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 86 upvotes, 74 comments
  28. Gonon: Building a Clock with No Numerals 21 upvotes, 15 comments
  29. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 159 upvotes, 179 comments
  30. AI and bots have officially taken over the internet 12 upvotes, 13 comments

Last generated 3:30pm, 30 March 2026. Source here.
